violations.text: Establishment's Rhode Island Food Protection Manager license is expired. PIC must re-instate. Application is in process of being submitted. New Violation. Correct By: 04/22/2025

The floors in the mop utility room are constructed of carpet which is not durable, easily cleanable, or non-absorbent. The materials for indoor floors must be durable, easily cleanable and nonabsorbent for areas subject to moisture such as food preparation areas, walk-in refrigerators, ware-washing areas, and toilet rooms. New Violation. Correct By: 05/24/2025

The walls in the mop utility room were observed in poor repair. The walls in the mop utility room are constructed of dry wall. This material is not durable. Materials for wall surfaces shall be smooth, durable, easily cleanable, and non-absorbent for areas subject to moisture such as food preparation areas, walk-in refrigerators, ware-washing areas, and toilet rooms New Violation. Correct By: 05/24/2025

The floor and wall junctures in the mop utility room do not have coving. The floor and wall junctures must be coved and closed to no larger than 1/32nd of an inch. New Violation. Correct By: 05/24/2025
Establishment's Rhode Island Food Protection Manager license is expired. PIC must re-instate. PIC states class and test is scheduled. New Violation. Correct By: 04/22/2025

Deceased large rodent was observed in mop utility room. Dead rodents, birds, and insects must be removed promptly from the facilities to ensure clean and sanitary facilities and to preclude exacerbating the situation by allowing carcasses to attract other pests. Carcass must be removed. New Violation. Correct By: 03/24/2025

The presence of large rodents is not controlled as evidenced by gnawed pink insulation, openings in dry wall, deceased rodent and rodent droppings (localized in mop utility room). Inspector unable to assess exterior perimeter of building due to weather. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/11/2025

Accumulation of organic matter observed at exterior of facility near side door. Uninstalled sink observed used to aid in straining organic matter (such as produce scraps). Uncovered barrels of produce waste observed stored near side door. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions and attractants for pests. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/14/2025

The floors in mop utility room have an accumulation of residue and pest excrement (large rodent droppings). The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 03/12/2025

The floors in the mop utility room are constructed of carpet which is not durable, easily cleanable, or non-absorbent. The materials for indoor floors must be durable, easily cleanable and nonabsorbent for areas subject to moisture such as food preparation areas, walk-in refrigerators, ware-washing areas, and toilet rooms. New Violation. Correct By: 05/24/2025

The walls in the mop utility room were observed in poor repair. The walls in the mop utility room are constructed of dry wall. This material is not durable. Materials for wall surfaces shall be smooth, durable, easily cleanable, and non-absorbent for areas subject to moisture such as food preparation areas, walk-in refrigerators, ware-washing areas, and toilet rooms. New Violation. Correct By: 05/24/2025

The floor and wall junctures in the mop utility room do not have coving. The floor and wall junctures must be coved and closed to no larger than 1/32nd of an inch. New Violation. Correct By: 05/24/2025
Establishment's Rhode Island Food Protection Manager license is expired. PIC must re-instate. Application provided during time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 04/22/2025

The presence of large rodents is not controlled as evidenced by gnawed equipment, nesting materials, opening in dry wall and rodent droppings (localized in mop utility room). Opening also observed at exterior of building. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 04/11/2025

Accumulation of organic matter observed at exterior of facility near side door. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions and attractants for pests. New Violation. Correct By: 03/14/2025

The floors under shelving in back storage room and in mop utility room have an accumulation of residue and pest excrement. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 03/12/2025
